howItWorks.intro: This lumber cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Board Feet = (Thickness × Width × Length in inches) ÷ 144
Total Board Feet = BF per Board × Number of Boards
Total Cost = Total Board Feet × Price per Board Foot
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a board foot?
faq.answer: A board foot (BF) is 144 cubic inches of wood — equivalent to a board 1 inch thick × 12 inches wide × 12 inches long. Hardwood lumber is priced per board foot.

faq.question: How much does hardwood lumber cost?
faq.answer: Common hardwoods: Poplar $3-5/BF, Red Oak $5-8/BF, Cherry $7-10/BF, Walnut $10-15/BF, Maple $5-8/BF. Exotic woods can exceed $20-50/BF.

faq.question: How do I calculate board feet?
faq.answer: Board Feet = (Thickness × Width × Length) ÷ 144, where all measurements are in inches. A 1" × 6" × 8' (96") board = 4 board feet.
